mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending power and driving positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and new housing starts signals broad-based demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of observations over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates supply-side capacity utilization, which historically compresses inflation expectations and supports equity valuations, yielding positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive tightening, which raises discount rates and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances following a 0.25pp rate hike within a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in the unemployment rate and increases in nonfarm payrolls confirm labor market healing, which sustains consumer demand and drives positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS predicts negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of historical windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and auto sales reflect accelerating consumer expenditure, which directly boosts corporate cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS changes remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, persistent CPI growth forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both trend upward.
